steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content: Exactly one distinct real solution means the quadratic can be written as a perfect square equal to $0$. Check which choice has a repeated factor.

steps.title: Identify the repeated-root choice
steps.content: Choice A crosses the x-axis twice, choice B never reaches the x-axis, and choice C crosses twice. The correct answer is choice D, $x^{2}-14x+49=0$, which is equivalent to $(x-7)^2=0$ and touches the x-axis once at $x=7$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Because $(x-7)^2=0$ has exactly one solution, $x=7$, D is correct.
